headline: Energy stocks rise on oil prices, BlackRock consortium to acquire AES
summary: Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman tracks several of the day's top trending stock tickers, including energy stocks moving higher on spiking oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F), shares of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) getting a boost amid escalations in the Middle East after the US bombing of Iran over the weekend, and BlackRock-owned (BLK) Global Infrastructure Partners leading a consortium to acquire AES (AES) for $10.7 billion. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: AES Plummets On Buy Out News. BlackRock, EQT To Take Utility Company Private
summary: BlackRock's infrastructure investment arm, Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP), and the Swedish investment firm EQT will acquire the utility company AES.  GIP and EDT will buy AES for $15 a share, bringing the deal's total equity value to $10.7 billion, according to a company statement.  The California Public Employees' Retirement System ("CalPERS") and Qatar Investment Authority are c0-underwriters in the deal.

raw.headline: AES Plummets On Buy Out News. BlackRock, EQT To Take Utility Company Private
raw.summary: BlackRock's infrastructure investment arm, Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP), and the Swedish investment firm EQT will acquire the utility company AES.  GIP and EDT will buy AES for $15 a share, bringing the deal's total equity value to $10.7 billion, according to a company statement.  The California Public Employees' Retirement System ("CalPERS") and Qatar Investment Authority are c0-underwriters in the deal.

headline: AES to Be Taken Private in $33 Billion Deal. Why the Stock Is Falling Sharply.
summary: AES  the utilities and clean energy company, is being taken private by an investor consortium that includes an  EQT Group  infrastructure fund and  BlackRock  -owned Global Infrastructure Partners.  The consortium, which also includes the Qatar Investment Authority and the California Public Employees’ Retirement System, will pay $15 a share for the world’s largest supplier of clean energy to corporations.  AES stock fell 17% to $14.40 in premarket trading Monday.

AES stock fell 17% to $14.40 in premarket trading Monday.

headline: Consortium Led by Global Infrastructure Partners and EQT Agrees to Acquire AES
summary: The AES Corporation (NYSE: AES) ("AES" or "the Company"), Global Infrastructure Partners ("GIP"), a part of BlackRock, and the EQT Infrastructure VI fund ("EQT"), along with co-underwriters California Public Employees' Retirement System ("CalPERS") and Qatar Investment Authority ("QIA") (collectively "the Consortium"), today announced they have entered into a definitive agreement under which the Consortium will acquire AES for $15.00 per share in cash, representing a total equity value of $10.7
